reviews.review_description: The photos are 3d but not 360 degrees.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear you're unhappy!   I don't believe I promised 360 photos anywhere in the description and the screenshots should have given that away pretty quick.   As always, you can request a refund if you didn't get what you were expecting.

reviews.review_description: Looking at the photos of Mars in 3D was fascinating but it was the final image that left me awestruck. I really got a feel of what it would be like to stand on Mars.  Enjoyed it very much thank you
For all the vr fans who like space

reviews.review_title: Awesome if you're interested in mars
reviews.review_description: This really drew home what the title is saying, that mars is an actual place that exists and this is one of the few ways to experience it almost like you are there. If you don't like looking at real Nasa photos because they are too low quality and prefer something fabricated then this is probably not for you but I would expect anyone really interested in space and mars to be delighted by this.

But what IS there is fantastic. If your a Mars fan it's worth getting. The geology is amazing and the music is a nice accompanyment. I can spend quite a bit of time in the finale. One day my friends...one day...
Best images I have ever seen of Mars.

I thought when I bought it that it would be more interesting.  The pictures were repetitive.  I actually expected that but I though there would be more background information then the location where the picture was taken.  The music was nice but got repetitive after a few minutes.   I believe I would have been just as entertained by going to NASA using the Oculus browser and looking at their pictures directly.

Hey DrashVR. I'm using Samsung S8+ and the app only shows a black screen, then after about 2 mins starts playing music. I left it for a while and no game loaded after that. I'm not going to ask for a refund because I am actually looking forward to using this and see what you've done :)

Update
Sorry for the delay, I just had my phone screen repaired. Thank you very much, the photos and 3d effect is fantastic!

developer_response.body: Great to hear you were able to fix your phone!  :)

reviews.review_description:
I was disappointed that the last and reportedly, the most spectacular photo did not open. 

I tried several times... you are forced to go through each all over again (very poor design). 

Loses 2 stars. One for the way too exaggerated depth in many scenes. Why? One because I can’t see the last image on my Oculus Go 64gb. :(

Note: this not a game. It’s basically a collection of 3D photos from Mars.

The pictures are really beautiful! But there is one problem with the convergence of the 2 separate views of the 3d panoramas, what results in an exhausting effort for the eyes and everthing seems to be to small, f.ex. the rover of the wonderful last panorama. That is so sad, because i like these kind of apps. Maybe the developer does an update to fix the problems...

developer_response.body:
Hi I'm sorry to hear you had a poor experience, but am left confused as to how to improve.
Audio: Can you explain further?  Not experiencing any issues on my end.
3D images: These are straight from the Mars Reconaissance Orbiter and a couple of the rovers on the ground, and they look pretty darn great to me.  Is it possible you're not keeping your head level?  Is it the black and white that you don't like?  They don't come in color so not much to do about that there.
Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
Folks it's not star wars it's Mars you know real image's, have you any idea what it takes to see these kind of images
 If you want star wars and light sabers look elsewhere but kudus to the Developer and great music well done.

When I first saw the title for this app, I was a tad confused. Of course it’s a real place, I thought. I wondered what the emphasis was. After having spent significant time with it, it seems apparent now. The focus here is on the educational. It’s not a game; there is no wave-shooting, cockpit-view navigation, aliens, or even movement. Being aware of that fact up front will hopefully avoid feeling let down if that is what you seek. If, however, you are looking for an above-average learning experience, this is for you. What it does provide you with are some truly excellent static shots of the planet’s surface and its numerous terrains and a vast wealth of knowledge. There is a LOT of information provided here. Once you navigate all of the pictures and text captions, you arrive at the final money shot, which many reviewers have already commented on. It is definitely a jaw-dropper; I can’t recall the last time I spent so many minutes just absorbing one single image. If you find yourself wondering throughout the experience where the VR component comes into play, this 3D panoramic view of the landscape answers that question emphatically.

So, yes, this title tells us that mars is a real place, not one borne of imagination. It has so many interesting qualities that are facts, not science fiction. That is the app’s inherent strength, and is worth your time and money, especially if you can find it on sale. The one-star deduction is due to a lack of dynamics. Imagine visiting a museum where you just stand in one place for the entire exhibition, and you can imagine the feeling of limitation and constraint. For as much obvious work that the developer has put into it, it could greatly benefit from some differentiated environments and feelings of movement. An app about space should not be devoid of space. Otherwise, I highly recommend it. I learned quite a bit, and it’s nice that VR as a technology can enhance such a meaningful practice.
Nice collection of stereographic (3D) imagery and panoramas of Mars
Nice images, although a more direct method of exploring would have been nice. Isn't there a Google Mars? If so, connecting that up would be a good addition.
